title: Cybersecurity Awareness Education
verbatim: A financial institution must provide adequate and regular technology and cybersecurity awareness education for all staff in undertaking their respective roles and measure the effectiveness of its education and awareness programs. This cybersecurity awareness education must be conducted at least annually and must reflect the evolving cyber threat landscape and emerging risks. Where appropriate, the financial institution shall also include its third party service providers in their relevant training as outlined in paragraph 10.48(i).
translation: A financial institution must provide all staff with adequate and regular technology and cybersecurity awareness education relevant to their roles, and must measure the effectiveness of those programmes. The education must be conducted at least annually and must reflect the evolving cyber threat landscape and emerging risks. Where appropriate, third-party service providers must also be included in relevant training.

artifacts.name: Security Awareness Program
artifacts.description: Documented security awareness program covering all personnel with system access

artifacts.name: Training Materials
artifacts.description: Annual cybersecurity awareness training content and materials

artifacts.name: Training Completion Report
artifacts.description: Report tracking training completion rates across all personnel

title: Continuous Training for Technology Staff
verbatim: A financial institution must provide adequate and continuous training for staff involved in technology operations, cybersecurity and risk management in order to ensure that the staff are competent and suitably certified to effectively perform their roles and responsibilities.
translation: Staff working in technology operations, cybersecurity, and risk management must receive adequate and ongoing training to maintain competence and obtain relevant certifications for their roles.
evidence: Continuous training program for technology and cybersecurity staff
Training records and attendance logs for technology operations personnel
Staff certification records (e.g., CISSP, CISM, OSCP, vendor certifications)
Competency assessments and skills gap analyses

artifacts.name: Technology Staff Training Program
artifacts.description: Documented continuous training program for technology operations, cybersecurity, and risk management staff

artifacts.name: Staff Certification Records
artifacts.description: Records of professional certifications held by technology and cybersecurity staff

artifacts.name: Competency Assessment Report
artifacts.description: Periodic assessments of staff competency and training effectiveness for technology roles

title: Board Training on Technology Developments
verbatim: A financial institution must provide its board members with regular training and information on technology developments to enable the board to effectively discharge its oversight role.
translation: Board members must receive regular training and briefings on technology developments so they can effectively oversee the institution's technology strategy, risks, and governance.
evidence: Board technology training schedule and calendar
Training materials and briefing decks presented to the board
Board meeting minutes recording technology training sessions
Attendance records for board technology briefings

artifacts.name: Board Technology Training Program
artifacts.description: Program for providing regular technology training and briefings to board members

artifacts.name: Board Technology Briefing Materials
artifacts.description: Presentation materials on technology developments provided to board members

artifacts.name: Board Training Attendance Records
artifacts.description: Records of board member attendance at technology training sessions
